content: As the title says. Is there another way to make rustdoc error on warn? I looked at the makefiles and thin that it might be a bug? -Dwarnings is included in KBUILD_RUSTFLAGS, but I don't think these are passed to rustdoc.
Please read https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20240519211235.589325-1-ojeda@kernel.org/ and consider replying saying that you would like to see it.
Thanks :folded_hands: Will read and test.
Clippy just adds more potential warnings, which are affected by -Dwarnings.

While we are waiting for those patches to land I got this:

          clippyDriver = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "clippy-driver" ''
            ${rustToolchain}/bin/clippy-driver \
              -Dwarnings \
              $@
          '';
          rustdoc = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "rustdoc" ''
            ${rustToolchain}/bin/rustdoc \
              -Dwarnings \
              $@
          '';

Seems to be the only way to add flags to rustdoc and clippy?

Andreas Hindborg dedi:

While we are waiting for those patches to land I got this:

          clippyDriver = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "clippy-driver" ''
            ${rustToolchain}/bin/clippy-driver \
              -Dwarnings \
              $@
          '';
          rustdoc = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "rustdoc" ''
            ${rustToolchain}/bin/rustdoc \
              -Dwarnings \
              $@
          '';

Seems to be the only way to add flags to rustdoc and clippy?

Hello, so injecting global flags directly into Rust toolchain for example clippy-driver is ​​not currently supported natively right? So overriding it with writeShellScriptBin is a common practice. for example:

let
tools = [
{ name = "clippy-driver"; cmd = "clippy-driver"; flags = "-Dwarnings"; }
{ name = "rustdoc"; cmd = "rustdoc"; flags = "-Dwarnings"; }
];
in
builtins.listToAttrs (map (tool:
{
name = tool.name;
value = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin tool.name ''
exec ${rustToolchain}/bin/${tool.cmd} ${tool.flags} "$@"
'';
}) tools)
I'm not sure if it is common. But it was just an easy way to add this flag while waiting for this series. Not sure if it will ever merge.

Ozgur Karatas said:

let
tools = [
{ name = "clippy-driver"; cmd = "clippy-driver"; flags = "-Dwarnings"; }
{ name = "rustdoc"; cmd = "rustdoc"; flags = "-Dwarnings"; }
];
in
builtins.listToAttrs (map (tool:
{
name = tool.name;
value = pkgs.writeShellScriptBin tool.name ''
exec ${rustToolchain}/bin/${tool.cmd} ${tool.flags} "$@"
'';
}) tools)

I can't follow this particular piece of nix expression. But what I did was use a shell script to inject the flag some times.
I am currently using this flake for kernel development. It is sort of ad-hoc, but it works well for me.
